articles.description: As a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship is set to arrive at Granadilla port in Tenerife, Spain on Sunday morning, the WHO, Spanish authorities and cruise company Oceanwide Expedition are coordinating the disembarkation of passengers and some crew on ground.

articles.title: Concerns grow over more gray whale deaths in Bay Area and climate change's contribution

articles.title: Storm expected to weaken, trigger rains in Visayas, Mindanao on Sunday
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines – Tropical Storm Caloy (international name: Hagupit) is forecast to weaken to a tropical depression but its trough or extension may trigger rains in the Visayas and Mindanao on Sunday evening, the state weather bureau said. “Caloy will not have a direct impact on any part of the country, but later its trough or extension could possibly bring rains to parts of Visayas and Mindanao,” Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) weather specialist Veronica Torres said in Filipino in a 5 a.m. update. READ: Tropical Storm ‘Hagupit’ enters PAR, given local name ‘Caloy’ Caloy was last [...]... Keep on reading: Storm expected to weaken, trigger rains in Visayas, Mindanao on Sunday

articles.title: Solar project reshapes Aeta lands in Zambales
articles.description: (First of 3 parts) SAN MARCELINO, Zambales — On clear mornings in this upland town, Mapanuepe Lake shimmers against forested ridges as the country accelerates its shift toward renewable energy amid global fuel price volatility and growing energy security concerns. articles.title: Epidemiologist in Argentina's southernmost town rejects 'smear campaign' over hantavirus outbreak
articles.description: Argentina’s tourism-dependent province — Tierra del Fuego, home to the southernmost city of Ushuaia — on Friday rejected the idea that the deadly hantavirus outbreak aboard an Atlantic cruise ship may have emerged from its territory.

articles.title: ‘It could have been a second Great Fire’: how east London blaze showed scale of UK wildfire threat
articles.description: In record 40C heat on 19 July 2022, 18 homes were lost in village of Wennington – a signal for firefighters to adapt, but UK response remains fragmented When neighbours urged Lynn Sabberton and her partner, Terry, to flee from their home in Wennington one day in 2022, the couple weren’t sure they should bother. A fire was burning in their village, on the eastern edge of London, but Terry thought it was too far away to be a problem. Struggling with a lung disease made worse by the record 40C heat that day, 19 July , he was wearing only his underwear and refused to budge from his armchair. Lynn remembers two police officers kicking open their front door and shouting that it was time to go. Lynn pleaded to be allowed to get Terry some clothes and was bundled upstairs to find them. Could she grab some papers? No. Her purse? No. Her cat, Jack? Also no. Continue reading...
